channel.title: INmaster
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from INmaster, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Symptoms That May Point to Chronic Migraine
Ways to Manage Blue Light for Better Sleep
Best Fruits to Eat During Cancer Treatment
item.description: Fruits can support nutrition during cancer care. Learn which options may ease treatment side effects and offer potential protective benefits.

item.title: AI Is Here to Replace Nuclear Treaties. Scared Yet?
item.description: The last major nuclear arms treaty between the US and Russia just expired. Some experts believe a combination of satellite surveillance, AI, and human...

item.title: How Curling Became the Winter Olympics’ Favorite Fixation
item.description: If throwing stones and sweeping ice seem strange to you, the centuries-old history of the sport explains why it became so beloved.

item.title: Oticon Zeal Review: A High Bar (and Price) for Hearing Aids
item.description: These ultra-tiny hearing aids push the boundary for high-quality audio in a minuscule prescription package.

item.title: Iran’s Digital Surveillance Machine Is Almost Complete
item.description: After more than 15 years of draconian measures, culminating in an ongoing internet shutdown, the Iranian regime seems to be staggering toward its digi...

item.title: Good News in History, February 9
item.description: 152 years ago today, American Pulitzer Prize-winning Poet Amy Lowell was born. During a career that spanned just over a dozen years, she wrote and pub...

item.title: Bryte Balance Pro Smart Mattress Review: Sleeping Is Believing
item.description: The Bryte Balance Pro takes smart mattresses to the next level with adjustable coils and programmable massage settings.

item.title: Chicken Schnitzel
item.description: You guys are obsessed with my Pork Schnitzel, so I knew I had to make a chicken version! Thin-pounded, lightly…

item.title: The dirty little secret behind my carry-on-only travel strategy
item.description: Here's a look at my best practices for the laundry-averse and how they make packing for trips as long as two weeks totally doable in a carry-on.

item.title: How to choose a credit card for airport lounge access
item.description: If you want your credit card to include airport lounge access, you have some great options. Here, we break down how to choose your perfect match.

item.title: San Francisco’s pro-billionaire march draws dozens
item.description: A march supporting California’s billionaires didn’t exactly draw a huge crowd on Saturday.

item.title: Artist Uses Cremation Ashes to Create Unique Memorial Paintings With Personal Meanings
item.description: An artist is using cremation ashes in his paintings to create unique memorial landscapes that grieving families can personalize as a way to remember l...

item.title: ‘Crime-Fighting’ Llamas Catch Thief by Surrounding Him in a Field Until Cops Arrive
item.description: A would-be thief was caught by police after a herd of llamas cornered the suspect in a field until officers could arrive. It was dark outside when a m...

item.title: Okay, I’m slightly less mad about that ‘Magnificent Ambersons’ AI project
item.description: But this is still a bad idea.

item.title: Crypto.com places $70M bet on AI.com domain ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: The purchase rewrites the domain record books -- not that the crypto industry has ever been accused of restraint when it comes to spending.

item.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Is $16B enough to build a profitable robotaxi business?
item.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility — your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

item.title: Apes Show Ability to Imagine in ‘Tea Party’ Experiments, and Scientists are Very Excited
item.description: Apes share the human ability to imagine and pretend, suggests new research that included a series of tea party experiments. Scientists at Johns Hopkin...

item.title: ‘Extinct’ Snail Found in Alleyway Results in Species Pulled Back From the Brink: A ‘Once in a Career’ Moment
item.description: A button-sized snail once thought extinct has been officially saved after conservationists bred and released more than 100,000 into the wild. The grea...

item.title: Amazon’s ‘Melania’ documentary stumbles in second weekend
item.description: After a better-than-expected opening weekend in theaters, box office for Amazon’s “Melania”fell 67%.

item.title: Bilt 2.0 cards are now live — here's how to activate your card and set up your account
item.description: The new Bilt 2.0 credit cards are live; cardholders need to take these steps to activate their new cards and start earning points. Here's what you nee...

item.title: Lovebirds can get a marriage license at this airport pop-up in Las Vegas
item.description: The Las Vegas airport operates a temporary marriage license bureau each February.

item.title: Why lunch is the main meal in some countries

item.description: From trains to table manners, these are the lessons Tokyo quietly teaches travellers. They’re practical, cultural, and often invisible until you get t...

item.title: Jobless claims fall to lowest level since mid-May
